evals.prompt: Plan only: turn "add rate limiting to the public API" into a work plan for acme/api-server. Don't implement anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Uses epic-decomposition to create the Epic/Story/Task hierarchy, places every issue on the board via add_item_to_project, sets Kind/Type on each, reads Status before writing it, and stops after reporting the hierarchy without touching any code.

deterministic_checks.description: Places new issues on the board rather than leaving them off it
Phase 0 reads branch protection before any decomposition happens
Never hand-rolls a GraphQL call for something a plugin tool already covers
Plan-only run never opens a PR at all

expectations: Creates one Epic with Story/Task children via native sub-issues
Every created issue is placed on the board and gets a Kind/Type field value
Does not blindly overwrite a board Status without reading it first
Stops after the Phase 1 report; no code is written and no PR is opened

evals.prompt: Execute the plan for Epic #200 in acme/api-server: implement every Task, then open the PR.
evals.expected_output: Implements each Task on one shared branch, runs gates, opens a single PR via create_pull_request, classifies it, routes review (including Copilot), and confirms linkage via get_linked_issues rather than assuming Closes #N worked.

deterministic_checks.description: Opens the PR through the plugin tool, not gh pr create
Applies type/size/risk labels to the new PR
Confirms PR-to-issue linkage structurally instead of assuming it from PR body text
Never falls back to the raw gh CLI PR-creation path this pipeline replaces

deterministic_checks.command: python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open('transcript.md').read(); n=len(set(re.findall(r'checkout -b ([\w./-]+)', t))); print(n); sys.exit(0 if n<=1 else 1)"
deterministic_checks.description: Implements on at most one shared feature branch, not one branch per Task

expectations: All Task implementations land on a single shared branch
One PR is opened referencing every Task with Closes #N
classify_pull_request and add_pull_request_to_project are both called
Does not merge the PR without explicit user confirmation

evals.prompt: While implementing Task #205 you notice the existing retry logic silently swallows a timeout error. File it and keep going.
evals.expected_output: Invokes the file-bug skill rather than a hand-rolled create_issue call, and if the bug gets fixed before the PR opens, advances its lifecycle state instead of leaving it at its filed state.

deterministic_checks.description: Uses the file-bug skill for the discovered defect
file-bug's own duplicate check ran before a new issue was created

expectations: The discovered bug is filed through file-bug, not a standalone create_issue call
If resolved in the same session, its lifecycle state is advanced via set_lifecycle_state rather than left untouched
Task implementation continues after the bug is filed rather than stalling the pipeline

evals.prompt: PR #310 for Epic #200 just got merged. Close the loop.
evals.expected_output: Calls sync_linked_issues_project_field to stamp the post-merge board field across every linked issue, rolls Story/Epic Status to Done only after checking it isn't already there, and reports any skippedCrossRepo entries needing manual follow-up.

deterministic_checks.description: Uses the plugin's own post-merge field-sync tool
Checks current Status before writing Done rather than blindly overwriting it

expectations: sync_linked_issues_project_field is called after merge, never before
Any skippedCrossRepo issues are reported for manual follow-up, not silently dropped
Story/Epic Status rollup checks current state before writing rather than assuming it's still Todo/In Progress
